summary: Iter 7 EligeHub testing: PDF report endpoint /api/reports/appuntamenti.pdf (admin/docente/super_admin scoping, period day|week|month, docente_id filter, %PDF magic-byte verification) + frontend Settimana/Giorno toggle on /appuntamenti, day-list-view rendering, day-slot click → booking modal, PDF download buttons (toolbar pdf-day/week/month + dashboard dash-pdf-day/week/month) for both admin and docente roles, mobile 390x844. All 11 new backend tests PASS and all 35 prior iter2-4 regression tests still PASS. All specced frontend behaviors verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/reports/appuntamenti.pdf is clean: correct role-scoping (docente forced to own id, super_admin 403 via require_role('admin','docente')), 404 on missing docente, 400 on invalid period via _date_range_for, Content-Disposition filename uses start date so 'appuntamenti-day-YYYY-MM-DD.pdf' / 'appuntamenti-month-YYYY-MM-01.pdf' as expected. Magic bytes %PDF verified on all 4 calls (admin day/week/month + docente week).

Frontend downloadPdf uses fetch+blob+a.click() with Bearer header — correct approach since backend StreamingResponse uses 'inline' disposition; auth header cannot be set on plain <a href>. Network capture confirmed content-type=application/pdf and status 200 for both toolbar pdf-* and dashboard dash-pdf-* buttons.

Day view (data-testid=day-list-view) renders correctly: big '12/06' date + 'VENERDÌ' eyebrow, prenotato blocks with cliente nome and delete button, 'HH:MM/HH:MM — non disponibile' grey rows, green button day-slot-HH:MM for free slots. Slot click opens booking modal with prefilled dal=12:00 al=13:00 (60min slot for Marco Bianchi confirmed).
Day nav day-prev/day-today/day-next correctly update the date (verified '12/06 VENERDÌ' → '14/06 DOMENICA' after 2 clicks day-next).
Week-view regression PASS (docente-calendar still renders, Marco's Giulia Neri appointment still shown).
Mobile 390x844: day-list-view container renders correctly and is mobile-friendly (vertical list, max-w-md mx-auto).

Docente role: dashboard PDF buttons work — PDF download via dash-pdf-week returned 200 with %PDF magic. Backend correctly ignored any passed docente_id and scoped to logged-in docente (verified by test_pdf_docente_role_ignores_docente_id).

Pre-existing hydration warning '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' is caused by Emergent visual editor instrumentation injecting x-source-* spans into <option> children — cosmetic dev-only, not iter7-introduced. Same as iter5 report.

Initial query_selector for dash-pdf-day/dash-pdf-week returned MISSING but the buttons render correctly (subsequent click + network capture worked, returning 200/PDF). This is a Playwright timing artifact (dashboard renders progressively after stats fetch); not a real bug. All 3 dash-pdf-* buttons confirmed clickable and functional.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing demo data (Marco Bianchi + Giulia Neri appointments Ven 12 Jun 09-12).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 7 fully verified. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter7_reports.py covers PDF endpoint thoroughly (status, content-type, magic bytes, role-scoping, 404, 400, 403). Note that the previous iter test files (test_iter2/3/4_features.py) require REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var to be set when run from CLI: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://... pytest backend/tests/`. The new iter7 test has a fallback URL hard-coded, so it works without the env. Hydration warning on <option><span> is pre-existing/cosmetic — main agent already aware (iter5 report).
rca of the issue: No issues found. Implementation aligned with spec.
